messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following vehicle fleet tracking policy for GDPR compliance:

"Company vehicles are fitted with GPS trackers to monitor location and driving behaviour. Tracking is active during working hours only and is disabled outside of business hours. Data is used for route optimisation, fuel efficiency, and health and safety compliance. Employees are notified of tracking during onboarding. Location data is retained for 90 days. Driving behaviour scores are shared with line managers."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) is implied for operational and safety purposes, but not explicitly stated. A Legitimate Interest Assessment should be referenced given the intrusive nature of location tracking.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Driving behaviour data could reveal health conditions (e.g., erratic driving linked to medical episodes). No safeguard is stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Route optimisation, fuel efficiency, and health and safety compliance are clearly stated as distinct purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Tracking limited to working hours only is a strong minimisation measure.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] 90-day retention for location data stated. Retention for driving behaviour scores is not addressed.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] No data subject rights mentioned. The right to object (Art. 21) is particularly relevant for legitimate interest processing.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] GPS tracking system provider not disclosed as a processor.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. GPS and telematics providers may use non-EEA infrastructure.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (LIA not referenced), Art. 5 (driving score retention absent), Art. 15-21 (rights absent), Art. 28 (GPS provider not disclosed), Art. 46 (transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Document and reference a Legitimate Interest Assessment for GPS tracking
- Add retention period for driving behaviour scores
- Add a full data subject rights section prominently including the right to object
- Disclose the GPS and telematics system provider as a processor
- Address international transfer safeguards
- Confirm DPO requirement and appoint if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
